scenarios.title: Cultural Connections
themes: In This Region, They Have a Custom Of...

listening.receptive: Can analyze simple information and questions about familiar topics when spoken slowly and clearly.
listening.interactive: Can contribute to simple discussions about celebrations and customs in their community by sharing personal experiences and responding to peers' questions
learning_outcomes: Can follow audio narratives about Panamanian cultural events and show recognition by answering questions about key details
Can contribute to simple discussions about celebrations and customs in their community by sharing personal experiences and responding to peers' questions
Can follow a short story about a cultural tradition and recall main details

reading.reading: Can analyze a variety of short texts.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can accurately pronounce irregular present tense verbs ending in the third-person singular '-s', '-es', and '-ies' by producing the correct sounds /s/, /z/, and /ɪz/ in words related to cultural traditions
learning_outcomes: Can comprehend descriptions in simple texts about cultural traditions
Can summarize the main points of texts about national festivals by using text features like headings and captions

Can accurately pronounce irregular present tense verbs ending in the third-person singular '-s', '-es', and '-ies' by producing the correct sounds /s/, /z/, and /ɪz/ in words related to cultural traditions

speaking.productive: Can engage in simple discussions by asking and responding to questions, exchanging information, and providing brief explanations about familiar topics.
speaking.interactive: Can engage in discussions about cultural traditions by asking and responding to questions and using follow-up prompts
learning_outcomes: Can present about a personally significant tradition
Can engage in discussions about cultural traditions by asking and responding to questions and using follow-up prompts
Can ask and respond to questions about regional festivals by providing relevant details

writing.productive: Can collaboratively create short texts, such as diary entries, creative pieces, and class reports, demonstrating growing coherence.
writing.interactive: Can respond with simple feedback to peer writing about a cultural event
learning_outcomes: Can write a diary entry describing a cultural event in a coherent and organized manner
Can respond with simple feedback to peer writing about a cultural event
Can write a short paragraph sharing feelings about a cultural celebration

mediation.text: Can mediate peer understanding by summarizing information, interpreting visuals, and explaining ideas with examples.
mediation.communication: Can mediate for peers instructions related to cultural events by miming actions
learning_outcomes: Can simplify for peers a complex text about cultural traditions by using visual aids and encouraging questions
Can mediate for peers instructions related to cultural events by miming actions
Can simplify cultural descriptions in texts, using gestures and simple language

grammar: Present simple and past simple for describing traditions and historical facts
pronunciation: Practice pronunciation of word stress. Phonemic awareness: Pronunciation of irregular present tense verbs ending in third-person singular '-s', '-es', and '-ies', focusing on the correct sounds /s/, /z/, and /ɪz/

pragmatic: functions: Describing celebrations and responding to questions. Expressing preferences.
examples: They decorate their homes with the national flag and participate in the parades. What traditions are important in your community? My favorite tradition is the parade because...
sociolinguistic: Engage in class discussions about regional and cultural celebrations, responding to questions about traditions.

assessment_ideas: {'id': 'grade8_scenario1_project1', 'name': 'Cultural Scrapbook', 'category': 'Cultural Documentation', 'overview': 'Students create a scrapbook showcasing their favorite local or national cultural traditions using present simple and past simple tense.', 'skills': ['Cultural Exploration: Describing traditions and customs from the past and present', 'Communication: Talking about culture and traditions using descriptive language and everyday expressions'], 'technology': ['Scrapbook creation tools', 'Virtual museum design software', 'Video recording tools for interviews']}

{'id': 'grade8_scenario1_project2', 'name': 'Heritage Museum Tour', 'category': 'Cultural Preservation', 'overview': "Students design a museum tour to explore Panama's local cultural traditions using gerunds after prepositions.", 'skills': ['Cultural Exploration: Describing traditions and customs from the past and present', 'Communication: Talking about culture and traditions using descriptive language and everyday expressions'], 'technology': ['Scrapbook creation tools', 'Virtual museum design software', 'Video recording tools for interviews']}

{'id': 'grade8_scenario1_project3', 'name': 'Tradition Video Interviews', 'category': 'Oral History', 'overview': 'Students record interviews with family members about a cultural celebration using simple past tense and gerunds.', 'skills': ['Cultural Exploration: Describing traditions and customs from the past and present', 'Communication: Talking about culture and traditions using descriptive language and everyday expressions'], 'technology': ['Scrapbook creation tools', 'Virtual museum design software', 'Video recording tools for interviews']}

scenarios.title: The Power of Education
themes: This Trimester, I Am Learning About...
Next Trimester, I Plan to Learn...

listening.receptive: Can analyze simple information and questions about familiar topics when spoken slowly and clearly.
listening.interactive: Can follow and contribute to simple class discussions about education topics, showing comprehension of details
learning_outcomes: Can infer simple implicit information from spoken narratives about education-related activities by responding to guiding questions
Can follow and contribute to simple class discussions about education topics, showing comprehension of details
Can listen for specific details in conversations about future studies

reading.reading: Can analyze a variety of short texts.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can produce and differentiate between minimal pair sounds, accurately identifying and pronouncing distinct phonemes
learning_outcomes: Can analyze the structure of short educational texts
Can develop arguments based on reading about educational themes
Can produce and differentiate between minimal pair sounds, accurately identifying and pronouncing distinct phonemes

speaking.productive: Can engage in simple discussions by asking and responding to questions, exchanging information, and providing brief explanations about familiar topics.
speaking.interactive: Can engage in discussions about academic interests by asking and responding to questions
learning_outcomes: Can describe a personal academic challenge and resolution
Can engage in discussions about academic interests by asking and responding to questions
Can ask and answer questions about personal learning goals to express preferences and improvement areas

writing.productive: Can collaboratively create short texts, such as diary entries, creative pieces, and class reports, demonstrating growing coherence.
writing.interactive: Can write a summary of the main points learned in a history lesson
learning_outcomes: Can write a short report on a class learning activity
Can write a summary of the main points learned in a history lesson
Can edit a peer's short narrative for clarity

mediation.text: Can mediate peer understanding by summarizing information, interpreting visuals, and explaining ideas with examples.
mediation.communication: Can facilitate a discussion about team roles, using gestures and keywords
learning_outcomes: Can summarize key points from an educational video for study notes, using simple visuals and keywords
Can facilitate a discussion about team roles, using gestures and keywords
Can summarize audio content for study notes, using gestures to illustrate main ideas

pronunciation: Practice stress patterns in modals and continuous forms. Phonemic Awareness: Practice production and sound discrimination in minimal pair sounds of familiar words

pragmatic: functions: Discussing goals and asking about progress. Offering encouragement.
examples: I want to improve my math skills. How are you doing in science? You should keep practicing to get better.
sociolinguistic: Engage in discussions about personal and learning goals and offer relevant suggestions for improvement, respecting others opinions or thoughts.

assessment_ideas: {'id': 'grade8_scenario2_project1', 'name': 'Educational Presentation', 'category': 'Academic Reflection', 'overview': 'Students create a presentation on the importance of education using present continuous to describe ongoing learning and modals to express future goals.', 'skills': ['Educational Reflection: Describing ongoing learning and suggestions for improvement', 'Creative thinking: Describing personal and learning goals using creative tools and ideas'], 'technology': ['Podcast platforms', 'Vision board tools', 'Video editing software for vlogs']}

{'id': 'grade8_scenario2_project2', 'name': 'Learning Vision Board', 'category': 'Goal Setting', 'overview': 'Students create a vision board for their personal and learning goals using present continuous tense and modals of ability and desire.', 'skills': ['Educational Reflection: Describing ongoing learning and suggestions for improvement', 'Creative thinking: Describing personal and learning goals using creative tools and ideas'], 'technology': ['Podcast platforms', 'Vision board tools', 'Video editing software for vlogs']}

{'id': 'grade8_scenario2_project3', 'name': 'Educational Group Report', 'category': 'Collaborative Learning', 'overview': 'Students create a group report sharing their current learning experiences and future educational goals using gerunds after prepositions.', 'skills': ['Educational Reflection: Describing ongoing learning and suggestions for improvement', 'Creative thinking: Describing personal and learning goals using creative tools and ideas'], 'technology': ['Podcast platforms', 'Vision board tools', 'Video editing software for vlogs']}

scenarios.title: My Favorite Book/Movie

listening.receptive: Can analyze simple information and questions about familiar topics when spoken slowly and clearly.
listening.interactive: Can identify key points shared by classmates when listening to explanations or participating in discussions
learning_outcomes: Can follow audio narratives by responding to questions in discussions about movies and books
Can identify key points shared by classmates when listening to explanations or participating in discussions
Can answer simple questions about favorite genres after listening to peers

reading.reading: Can analyze a variety of short texts.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can accurately identify and produce sounds in minimal pairs
learning_outcomes: Can interpret characters' motives in short story descriptions by answering questions about key decisions and actions
Can identify the main ideas of persuasive texts about books or movies
Can accurately identify and produce sounds in minimal pairs

speaking.productive: Can engage in simple discussions by asking and responding to questions, exchanging information, and providing brief explanations about familiar topics.
speaking.interactive: Can participate in class discussions by asking and answering questions about favorite books
learning_outcomes: Can give a brief overview of a favorite movie in class discussions
Can participate in class discussions by asking and answering questions about favorite books
Can share opinions in group discussions about favorite books

writing.productive: Can collaboratively create short texts, such as diary entries, creative pieces, and class reports, demonstrating growing coherence.
writing.interactive: Can provide feedback on a peer's description of a movie
learning_outcomes: Can compose a short, creative piece inspired by a favorite book using simple details to describe key elements
Can provide feedback on a peer's description of a movie
Can compose a simple review, sharing why a book or movie is enjoyable

mediation.text: Can mediate peer understanding by summarizing information, interpreting visuals, and explaining ideas with examples.
mediation.communication: Can clarify misunderstood social cues using facial expressions and gestures to show understanding
learning_outcomes: Can mediate a simple disagreement between peers about a favorite movie using gestures
Can clarify misunderstood social cues using facial expressions and gestures to show understanding
Can mediate discussions about movie genres using gestures to emphasize different preferences

grammar: Past simple for describing plot and actions
pronunciation: Practice production and sound discrimination in minimal pair sounds. Focus on distinguishing between /ʊ/ in 'full' and /uː/ in 'fool'

pragmatic: functions: Expressing opinions and giving reasons. Asking for recommendations. Responding to feedback.
examples: My favorite movie is about space exploration. I recommend this book because it's exciting. What book do you recommend?
sociolinguistic: Participate in peer discussions about entertainment preferences, respecting different opinions and offering constructive feedback.

assessment_ideas: {'id': 'grade8_scenario3_project1', 'name': 'Book/Movie Review Blog', 'category': 'Digital Writing', 'overview': 'Students create a blog reviewing their favorite books or movies using present simple and past simple tenses to describe and explain their preferences.', 'skills': ['Critical Thinking: Analyzing and evaluating entertainment content', 'Communication: Expressing and justifying opinions clearly'], 'technology': ['Blog platforms', 'Video review software', 'Digital presentation tools']}

{'id': 'grade8_scenario3_project2', 'name': 'Character Analysis Presentation', 'category': 'Literary Analysis', 'overview': 'Students create presentations analyzing characters from their favorite stories, explaining motives and making connections.', 'skills': ['Critical Thinking: Analyzing and evaluating entertainment content', 'Communication: Expressing and justifying opinions clearly'], 'technology': ['Blog platforms', 'Video review software', 'Digital presentation tools']}

{'id': 'grade8_scenario3_project3', 'name': 'Recommendation Video', 'category': 'Persuasive Communication', 'overview': 'Students create videos recommending books or movies to classmates using persuasive language and clear explanations.', 'skills': ['Critical Thinking: Analyzing and evaluating entertainment content', 'Communication: Expressing and justifying opinions clearly'], 'technology': ['Blog platforms', 'Video review software', 'Digital presentation tools']}

scenarios.title: Sustainable Living
themes: In the Past, People Didn't Recycle as Much as They Do Now

listening.receptive: Can analyze simple information and questions about familiar topics when spoken slowly and clearly.
listening.interactive: Can respond to questions about sustainability practices in class discussions
learning_outcomes: Can infer implicit information from audio about environmental topics
Can respond to questions about sustainability practices in class discussions
Can listen for specific details about environmental changes

reading.reading: Can analyze a variety of short texts.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can accurately pronounce past tense verbs ending in '-ed'
learning_outcomes: Can analyze informational texts about environmental issues
Can interpret motives in stories about sustainability
Can accurately pronounce past tense verbs ending in '-ed'

speaking.productive: Can engage in simple discussions by asking and responding to questions, exchanging information, and providing brief explanations about familiar topics.
speaking.interactive: Can engage in problem-solving discussions about environmental issues
learning_outcomes: Can present findings about sustainable practices
Can engage in problem-solving discussions about environmental issues
Can suggest ways to reduce waste and save resources

writing.productive: Can collaboratively create short texts, such as diary entries, creative pieces, and class reports, demonstrating growing coherence.
writing.interactive: Can write instructions for sustainable living
learning_outcomes: Can write a short report on environmental practices
Can write instructions for sustainable living
Can edit peers' writing about environmental topics

mediation.text: Can mediate peer understanding by summarizing information, interpreting visuals, and explaining ideas with examples.
mediation.communication: Can interpret graphs about pollution or recycling
learning_outcomes: Can explain environmental concepts using simple analogies
Can interpret graphs about pollution or recycling
Can help peers understand sustainability data

grammar: Past simple for comparing past and present practices
pronunciation: Practice pronunciation of past tense verbs ending '-ed' (/t/, /d/, /ɪd/ sounds)

pragmatic: functions: Making suggestions. Comparing past and present. Giving instructions.
examples: You can save water by turning off the tap. In the past, people didn't recycle as much. We should use less plastic.
sociolinguistic: Discuss environmental responsibility and engage in collaborative problem-solving about sustainability.

{'id': 'grade8_scenario4_project2', 'name': 'Before and After Comparison', 'category': 'Historical Analysis', 'overview': 'Students create presentations comparing environmental practices from the past to the present using past simple.', 'skills': ['Environmental Awareness: Understanding and promoting sustainable practices', 'Problem-Solving: Finding solutions to environmental challenges'], 'technology': ['Infographic creation tools', 'Video editing software', 'Social media platforms']}

{'id': 'grade8_scenario4_project3', 'name': 'Eco-Tips Guide', 'category': 'Instructional Writing', 'overview': 'Students create a guide with practical tips for sustainable living using imperatives and clear instructions.', 'skills': ['Environmental Awareness: Understanding and promoting sustainable practices', 'Problem-Solving: Finding solutions to environmental challenges'], 'technology': ['Infographic creation tools', 'Video editing software', 'Social media platforms']}

scenarios.title: Career Goals
themes: I Want to Be a Doctor Because...
To Become a Doctor, First I Will Need To...

listening.receptive: Can analyze simple information and questions about familiar topics when spoken slowly and clearly.
listening.interactive: Can participate in discussions about future professions
learning_outcomes: Can get the gist of dialogues about career choices
Can participate in discussions about future professions
Can understand key information about career requirements

reading.reading: Can analyze a variety of short texts.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can accurately produce minimal pair sounds
learning_outcomes: Can analyze texts about different careers
Can summarize information about job requirements
Can accurately produce minimal pair sounds

speaking.productive: Can engage in simple discussions by asking and responding to questions, exchanging information, and providing brief explanations about familiar topics.
speaking.interactive: Can explain steps needed to achieve career goals
learning_outcomes: Can describe career aspirations and reasons
Can explain steps needed to achieve career goals
Can respond to questions about future plans

writing.productive: Can collaboratively create short texts, such as diary entries, creative pieces, and class reports, demonstrating growing coherence.
writing.interactive: Can write a plan outlining steps to achieve a career
learning_outcomes: Can write about career goals and aspirations

mediation.text: Can mediate peer understanding by summarizing information, interpreting visuals, and explaining ideas with examples.
mediation.communication: Can help peers understand different career paths
learning_outcomes: Can facilitate discussions about career options
Can help peers understand different career paths
Can interpret career-related information for classmates

grammar: Modals for necessity (need to, have to, must)
pronunciation: Practice stress patterns in multi-syllable career words and modal verbs

pragmatic: functions: Expressing future plans. Explaining reasons. Describing requirements.
examples: I want to be a doctor because I like helping people. To become a doctor, first I will need to study medicine. My goal is to work in a hospital.
sociolinguistic: Discuss career aspirations respectfully and support peers' goals regardless of chosen profession.

{'id': 'grade8_scenario5_project2', 'name': 'Career Interview Project', 'category': 'Research and Communication', 'overview': 'Students interview professionals about their careers and create presentations about what they learned.', 'skills': ['Goal Setting: Planning and articulating future aspirations', 'Research: Investigating career requirements and pathways'], 'technology': ['Mind mapping software', 'Presentation tools', 'Video interview platforms']}

scenarios.title: Learning from Our Mistakes
themes: What Can I Learn from This Experience?
Next Time, I Will Study Harder to Avoid...

listening.receptive: Can analyze simple information and questions about familiar topics when spoken slowly and clearly.
listening.interactive: Can contribute to discussions about personal challenges
learning_outcomes: Can identify main ideas in discussions about learning experiences
Can contribute to discussions about personal challenges
Can understand stories about overcoming difficulties

reading.reading: Can analyze a variety of short texts.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can accurately produce minimal pair sounds
learning_outcomes: Can read and understand texts about learning from mistakes
Can identify arguments in texts about personal growth
Can accurately produce minimal pair sounds

speaking.productive: Can engage in simple discussions by asking and responding to questions, exchanging information, and providing brief explanations about familiar topics.
speaking.interactive: Can discuss how to improve in the future
learning_outcomes: Can explain what was learned from a mistake
Can discuss how to improve in the future
Can share personal experiences of overcoming challenges

writing.productive: Can collaboratively create short texts, such as diary entries, creative pieces, and class reports, demonstrating growing coherence.
writing.interactive: Can write a reflection on mistakes and improvements
learning_outcomes: Can write about a learning experience
Can write a reflection on mistakes and improvements

mediation.text: Can mediate peer understanding by summarizing information, interpreting visuals, and explaining ideas with examples.
mediation.communication: Can explain growth mindset concepts simply
learning_outcomes: Can help peers understand the value of mistakes
Can explain growth mindset concepts simply
Can mediate discussions about learning challenges

grammar: Future tense for plans and resolutions
Modal verbs for advice (should, could, might)
pronunciation: Practice minimal pair sounds in common reflection vocabulary

pragmatic: functions: Reflecting on experiences. Making future plans. Giving and receiving advice.
examples: What can I learn from this experience? Next time, I will study harder to avoid mistakes. I should have asked for help.
sociolinguistic: Create a supportive environment for discussing mistakes and encourage growth mindset.

scenarios.title: A Day Without Technology

learning_outcomes: Can understand discussions about technology use

learning_outcomes: Can read and analyze texts about technology use

learning_outcomes: Can summarize different viewpoints on technology
Can help peers understand balanced technology use

grammar: Modal verbs for possibility and ability
pronunciation: Practice pronunciation of past tense verbs and technology vocabulary

examples: One advantage of technology is... On the other hand, without technology we can... I think we depend too much on our phones.
sociolinguistic: Engage in balanced discussions about technology use and respect different viewpoints on digital habits.
